name: Lienchiang County (Taiwan)
description: A white field charged at the centre with the Lienchiang County emblem.
history: Lienchiang County (Lianjiang Xian, often called Matsu after its main island group) is the smallest county in Taiwan by both area and population, comprising a chain of islands at the mouth of the Min River. It is the only part of historical Lienchiang County (Fujian Province) that remained under Republic-of-China administration after 1949. The current county flag dates from the late 20th century, reflecting the islands' fishing heritage and strategic maritime location.

features.symbolism: stylised characters 連江 with motifs of waves and a fishing boat, expressing the Matsu islands' fishing heritage and maritime location at the mouth of the Min River
